notes: implemented_not_surfaced_count is seeded from SALE-ORD-027 in the March 17 evaluation pack
code_to_live_lag_days dropped to 0 once staging build-mmwp9o9e proved the deployed Add Item focus repair and preserved the cleared AG Grid license state on March 18, 2026
staging_failures includes the March 18, 2026 queue-route crash on build-mmvlul6k at /sales?tab=orders&surface=sheet-native&orderId=627 before the shared-runtime hardening fix was deployed
local full-gate verification is green on repo states 3e205c7e and 0e2bb8ba; staging build-mmwp9o9e now carries the Add Item focus repair and preserves the cleared AG Grid license state

March 18, 2026 license investigation found that the live DigitalOcean app specs were missing VITE_AG_GRID_LICENSE_KEY even though .env.local had the key; staging deployment b0f31972-127c-4906-b2f2-a53cdb8d89be and production deployment 36eb5bb9-5678-47ea-9d61-eb940241d951 now carry the fix

Fresh staging build build-mmwdxj4j proved the route renders again but AG Grid still reports License Key Not Found, which traced the remaining blocker to Dockerfile build args not forwarding VITE_AG_GRID_LICENSE_KEY into the Vite build stage

Staging proof command pnpm proof:staging:orders-runtime:g2 now captures queue-route license health plus document-route duplicate, quick-add, delete, Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Shift+Enter, and Escape evidence under output/playwright/orders-runtime-g2/2026-03-18/

March 18, 2026 adversarial review kept SALE-ORD-019, SALE-ORD-020, SALE-ORD-021, SALE-ORD-022, SALE-ORD-029, SALE-ORD-031, and SALE-ORD-035 in partial because the current clipboard and failure-mode packet is still contradictory or not safely repeatable

A later March 18 isolated rerun on build-mmwp9o9e proved the Add Item focus repair is live, showed browser clipboard readback working again (`3\n4`), and rejected the earlier two-cell-range blocker because the correct live signal is `selectionSummaryBeforeValidPaste: 2 selected cells · 2 rows in scope`, not the focused-cell state alone

The repaired G2 harness now records `validPasteUsedTwoCellRange: true`, `validPasteMethod: keyboard`, and `quantityValuesAfterValidPaste: [3,4]`, which proves deterministic rectangular keyboard paste on staging without reopening a runtime defect theory

A fresh clean-writer rerun on March 18 also reconfirmed `duplicateDelta: 1`, `quickAddDelta: 1`, and `deleteReturnedToBaseline: true`, so TER-795 remains blocked only on fill-handle, sort/filter-safe targeting, clear-style action proof, and the remaining failure bundle rather than on TER-796 regression evidence

March 19, 2026 fill-handle probing on staging build-mmwp9o9e proved the native `.ag-fill-handle` is visible and enters `ag-dragging-fill-handle` during drag, but the quantity values still do not propagate (`[3,4,1,1]`), which turns SALE-ORD-022 into a stable live repro instead of a selector or selection-summary gap

March 19, 2026 local TER-795 work replaced the Orders document surface's default fill series with a deterministic vertical AG Grid `setFillValue` callback for approved fields and passed targeted vitest coverage, so the next evidence move is one narrow live fill verification instead of another broad proof rerun

March 19, 2026 degraded local boot probing proved the writer worktree can serve a production build without `DATABASE_URL` via `AUTO_MIGRATE_MODE=off SKIP_SEEDING=true PORT=3001 pnpm start`, and shell Playwright can now render the real sheet-native Orders document route by mocking `auth.me`, feature flags, and the required tRPC batches

The March 19, 2026 local sheet-native fill drag probe records `selectionSummaryBeforeDrag: 2 selected cells · 2 rows in scope`, `fillHandleVisible: true`, and `quantityValuesAfterDrag: [3,4,5,6]`, so the repaired fill path is browser-proven locally even though TER-795 remains blocked until a deployed build confirms the same behavior

A dedicated narrow deployed-build command now exists at `pnpm proof:staging:orders-fill-handle`, which isolates SALE-ORD-022 and avoids rerunning the broader G2 proof bundle when the next fresh build is available

That narrow probe was validated once against the current staging build build-mmwp9o9e and reproduces the still-live failure in isolation (`selectionSummaryBeforeDrag: 2 selected cells · 2 rows in scope`, `fillHandleVisible: true`, `bodyClassDuringDrag: ag-dragging-fill-handle`, `quantityValuesAfterDrag: [3,4,1,1]`), so the browser budget can stay spent here until a fresh deployed build exists

The probe itself is now hardened to reuse the main G2 selection-summary fallback, emit after-drag selection state plus console or page-error warnings, and accept either `PLAYWRIGHT_ORDERS_DRAFT_ID` or `ORDERS_RUNTIME_DRAFT_ID`, which removes the need for another custom shell probe on the next deployed-build check

March 19 continuation rechecked staging with `doctl apps list-deployments 62f2d9f8-3fb5-4576-9f7b-8dd91cf552a6` plus `curl https://terp-staging-yicld.ondigitalocean.app/version.json` and confirmed there is still no build newer than build-mmwp9o9e, so SALE-ORD-022 stays a deploy-blocked limitation until the local fill repair is shipped

The same continuation found and fixed a real sort/filter-safe row-targeting drift in the shared runtime by carrying `focusedRowId` through the selection contract and preserving fill writeback by row id; targeted vitest coverage and `pnpm check` both passed on that follow-up tranche

Claude adversarial review on March 19 kept SALE-ORD-031 at partial with a limitation note because the Orders document grid still disables sort/filter, so the new row-id safeguards are code-proven but not yet live-proven on a surface that exercises that path

A grep audit after the focusedRowId contract change confirmed the only in-repo runtime consumers of `PowersheetSelectionSet` are SpreadsheetPilotGrid, PowersheetGrid, and OrdersDocumentLineItemsGrid, plus their focused tests
